controlling_test: Fork interoperability should increase independent reviewability of claim, non-claim, reliance, and expansion boundaries without silently transferring truth, approval, compliance, admissibility, legal sufficiency, safety, runtime-control, or authority determinations across layers.
interoperability_doctrine: Fork does not create interoperability through authority transfer, semantic inheritance, or bilateral trust assumptions. Fork contributes to interoperability by preserving independently inspectable evidence-boundary records that make claims, non-claims, unresolved pointers, reliance propagation, and downstream expansion visible for later review.

layer.vendor_layer_descriptor: Out-of-band recomputable evidence-boundary infrastructure for AI-assisted workflows
layer.declaration_note: This manifest exposes Fork's claim boundary in GLM-readable form. It does not declare GLM certification, EVIDE compatibility, legal sufficiency, compliance approval, admissibility, safety certification, truth determination, runtime control, or authority transfer.

surfaces.operational_scope: Preserves what was requested, what AI produced, what humans reviewed, what claims were declared, what non-claims were preserved, what evidence was referenced, and what verification state was recorded when an AI-assisted workflow artifact or receipt is sealed.
does_not: Does not decide whether the workflow action was correct
Does not decide whether the workflow action was authorized
Does not decide whether the workflow action was compliant
Does not decide whether the workflow action was safe
Does not approve the workflow action

surfaces.operational_scope: Preserves declared claims, explicit non-claims, upstream reliance, rejected reliance, unresolved pointers, consumer-declared reliance, and downstream expansion signals across handoffs and composed artifacts.
does_not: Does not convert upstream claims into downstream claims
Does not remove or erase non-claims
Does not resolve unresolved pointers by implication
Does not authorize downstream expansion
Does not validate the authority basis for any expanded claim

surfaces.operational_scope: Records how downstream consumers state that they relied on, preserved, narrowed, expanded, rejected, or left unresolved specific upstream claims and non-claims.
does_not: Does not determine whether the reliance was reasonable
Does not determine whether the reliance was legally sufficient
Does not determine whether the reliance satisfied a policy or regulatory requirement
Does not determine whether the downstream consumer had authority to rely

surfaces.operational_scope: Allows independent recomputation of sealed record integrity, schema conformance, manifest constraints, digest consistency, boundary-preservation structure, and declared non-authority limitations.
does_not: Does not determine truth
Does not determine correctness
Does not determine safety
Does not determine compliance
Does not determine legal sufficiency
Does not determine admissibility
Does not determine institutional acceptance

surfaces.operational_scope: Makes visible whether composed governance artifacts preserve, narrow, expand, obscure, or fail to map their upstream claim and non-claim boundaries.
does_not: Does not certify composability with any external governance layer
Does not certify interoperability with EVIDE, GLM, or any adjacent framework
Does not decide which declaration prevails when governance declarations conflict
Does not adjudicate authority between layers

timing_axis.description: Fork operates out-of-band across record creation, claim-boundary preservation, reliance mapping, downstream expansion visibility, and later recomputation. Fork does not control execution, resolve admissibility, approve actions, determine truth, certify safety, determine compliance, validate legal sufficiency, or transfer authority.

does: Preserves recomputable evidence-boundary records for AI-assisted workflows
Records declared claims and explicit non-claims
Records evidence references and declared reliance relationships
Records unresolved pointers without treating them as resolved
Records downstream claim expansion signals when declared or structurally detectable
Supports later structural recomputation of sealed records, receipts, manifests, schemas, and bundles
Helps distinguish structural verification from truth, safety, compliance, approval, legal sufficiency, admissibility, and authority determinations
Helps make downstream boundary expansion inspectable when a downstream artifact declares, adds, transforms, or appears to rely on claims not preserved from the upstream boundary
does_not: Does not determine truth
Does not determine factual correctness
Does not determine legal sufficiency
Does not determine regulatory compliance
Does not certify safety
Does not approve decisions
Does not authorize execution
Does not resolve admissibility
Does not validate authority
Does not control runtime behavior
Does not block or permit production actions
Does not replace human, legal, audit, compliance, risk, or institutional review
Does not convert upstream claims into downstream claims
Does not make unresolved pointers resolved
Does not certify interoperability with adjacent governance layers
Does not claim compatibility with EVIDE or any specific external GLM manifest in this version

claims_boundary.authoritative_layer_claim: Fork preserves recomputable evidence-boundary records for AI-assisted workflows by structurally recording declared claims, explicit non-claims, evidence references, reliance relationships, unresolved pointers, downstream expansion signals, and later verification state without deciding truth, safety, compliance, approval, legal sufficiency, admissibility, execution permission, or authority.
explicit_non_claims: Fork does not determine truth
Fork does not determine factual correctness
Fork does not determine legal sufficiency
Fork does not determine regulatory compliance
Fork does not certify safety
Fork does not approve decisions
Fork does not authorize execution
Fork does not resolve admissibility
Fork does not validate authority
Fork does not control runtime behavior
Fork does not block or permit production actions
Fork does not replace human review
Fork does not replace legal review
Fork does not replace audit review
Fork does not replace compliance review
Fork does not replace risk review
Fork does not replace institutional governance review
Fork does not convert upstream claims into downstream claims
Fork does not erase upstream non-claims
Fork does not make unresolved pointers resolved
Fork does not certify interoperability with GLM, EVIDE, or any adjacent governance layer
Fork does not claim compatibility with EVIDE
Fork does not determine which governance declaration prevails when declarations conflict
claims_boundary.consumer_boundary_constraint: Consumers must not interpret Fork records, receipts, manifests, check outputs, verification results, structural passes, recomputation results, or preserved evidence-boundary artifacts as approval, authorization, truth certification, legal sufficiency, compliance certification, safety certification, admissibility determination, runtime permission, authority transfer, interoperability certification, or proof that downstream claim expansion is justified.
claims_boundary.non_claim_integrity_rule: A downstream Fork artifact that consumes or references an upstream claim-boundary record should preserve the upstream non-claims or explicitly declare any dropped, narrowed, unresolved, or expanded boundary behavior. Silent erasure of non-claims is outside Fork's declared safe-consumption posture.
claims_boundary.downstream_expansion_rule: A downstream artifact may add or expand claims only by declaring the expansion as downstream behavior and referencing its own authority basis or claim-boundary contract. Fork preservation of the expansion signal does not validate the expanded claim.

composition.composition_note: Fork may structurally preserve handoffs to or from adjacent governance layers, but this v0.1 declaration does not claim verified composability, reciprocal compatibility, or interoperability certification with any specific external manifest.
composition_assumptions: Adjacent layers remain responsible for their own declared claims and non-claims
Fork preservation of an adjacent layer's declaration does not validate that declaration
Fork recomputation of a record does not determine whether the record satisfies an adjacent layer's operational requirements
Fork can make declared overlap or expansion visible, but does not adjudicate which declaration controls
Mutual composability requires explicit review beyond this unilateral v0.1 declaration

machine_readable_status.declaration_status_note: Experimental declaration for machine-readable inspection of Fork's governance boundary. It is not a claim of GLM certification, EVIDE compatibility, external validation, production readiness, or institutional adoption.

public_anchors.publication_note: This repository path is the initial experimental publication surface. A canonical /.well-known/governance-layer-manifest.json publication should occur only after local Fork boundary checks and external GLM validator review.

glm_compatibility.non_breaking_addition_policy: minor version increment, backward compatible within major version
glm_compatibility.compatibility_note: This declaration uses GLM schema 1.3 fields for a single-layer manifest. It does not use organization_index and does not claim external validator approval beyond whatever structural checks a GLM tool performs.

manifest_digest.canonicalization_method: SHA-256 computed over the file as served, with manifest_digest.value set to the placeholder string <computed-at-publish-time> during computation.
